title: Icy Indulgent Vietnamese Kale Nem Nuong
description: A yeasty, fluffy, and indulgent Vietnamese dish featuring char-grilled kale, served with a refreshing sweet and spicy dip. Perfect for a lazy Sunday morning.

ingredients.name: Kale, chopped
ingredients.notes: Any variety of kale will work.

ingredients.name: Vietnamese rice flour
ingredients.notes: All-purpose flour can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Instant dry yeast
ingredients.notes: Active dry yeast can be used instead.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Granulated sugar can be used instead.

ingredients.name: Warm water
ingredients.notes: The water should be around 100F to 110F.

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.notes: Corn flour can be used instead.

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: Vegetable oil can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lime juice is recommended.

ingredients.name: Ginger, grated
ingredients.notes: Fresh ginger can be used instead.

ingredients.name: Chili flakes
ingredients.notes: Red pepper flakes can be used instead.

ingredients.name: Shredded carrots
ingredients.notes: Optional, for added color and texture.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped kale, Vietnamese rice flour, instant dry yeast, salt, sugar, and cornstarch. Stir until the ingredients are well combined.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes, until it becomes elastic and smooth.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a char-grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8-10 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin sheet, about 1/16 inch thick.
Place a sheet of dough on the preheated grill and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it is golden brown and slightly puffed.
Flip the nem nuong and cook for another 2-3 minutes, or until it is golden brown on the other side.
Repeat with the remaining dough pieces.
To make the sweet and spicy dip, combine the sesame oil, lime juice, ginger, and chili flakes in a bowl. Stir until the ingredients are well combined.
Add the shredded carrots and stir until they are well coated with the dip.
Serve the nem nuong with the sweet and spicy dip on the side, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
